page: Amazon Web Services c5ad.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7R32 processor. It features 64 virtual CPUs across 32 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. For storage, the instance is equipped with 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and it connects to the network with a baseline bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in database and cryptographic operations. The instance achieves high scores in Passmark database operations, Passmark encryption, and Redis SET operations, alongside strong performance in ray tracing. However, single-core CPU performance is poor, placing in the bottom 10% of stress-ng benchmarks, and large-scale LLM text generation is weak. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and software compilation fall within average ranges.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5ad.16xlarge offers a high-density compute profile with integrated high-speed local storage. The inclusion of 2400 GB of NVMe SSD storage provides qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring fast scratch space, avoiding external storage overhead. The primary tradeoff is the lower single-core performance compared to newer architectures, though its multi-core density and memory bandwidth remain balanced for parallel tasks.

This instance is suited for multi-threaded compute workloads, database operations, caching layers like Redis, and software compilation. The combination of dedicated AMD EPYC cores and local NVMe storage also makes it appropriate for data processing, web serving, and distributed analytics.
description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.16xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. It delivers 64 vCPUs, 128 GB of DDR4 memory, and 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 20 Gbps baseline network. Benchmarks show strong capabilities in database operations, Redis caching, and cryptography, though single-core CPU performance is relatively poor. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for parallel workloads requiring high-speed local storage without external disk overhead. It features 16 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX and SHA_NI instructions. This configuration is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, software compilation, and web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR4 RAM, and 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong database and cryptographic performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5ad.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 2400 GB NVMe SSD storage for database workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring sixty-four virtual cores, integrated NVMe SSD storage, and strong database and cryptographic performance.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7R32 processor
2400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage included
Strong performance in database operations and Redis caching
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for reliable data transfer
Optimized for multi-threaded compute, software compilation, and web serving
